title: Deloitte Graduate Program 2026 Applications Open: Launch Your Career with One of the World’s Leading Professional Services Firms
summary: Every successful career starts with the right opportunity, and Deloitte is offering aspiring professionals exactly that. Applications are now open for the 2026 Deloitte Graduate and Vacationer Programs , providing university students and recent graduates with the chance to develop future-ready skills, gain hands-on experience, and begin a rewarding professional journey with one of the world s leading consulting and professional services organizations. Whether you aspire to work in consulting, technology, audit, tax, strategy, engineering, or other professional disciplines, Deloitte s Graduate Program is designed to help talented individuals build meaningful careers while making a real impact from their very first day. Applications are open from 1 July 2026 until 22 July 2026 , so interested candidates are encouraged to apply before the deadline. About the Deloitte Graduate Program The Deloitte Graduate Program is designed for students who are completing their university studies during mid-to-late 2026 or graduates who have completed their degrees within the previous two years. Successful applicants will begin their careers with Deloitte in 2027. From the moment graduates join Deloitte, they become part of a diverse cohort of professionals representing various academic disciplines, backgrounds, and experiences. The program provides an inclusive environment where participants collaborate, learn together, and establish lasting professional relationships. Unlike many graduate programs that limit applicants based on their academic background, Deloitte recognizes that talent comes in many forms. Graduates are encouraged to explore different career pathways regardless of their degree specialization. For example, Deloitte welcomes: Technology graduates working in Tax Engineers contributing to Audit IT graduates joining Strategy Professionals from various disciplines across multiple business units Your university degree serves as a starting point rather than a limitation. Why Choose Deloitte? Deloitte believes that exceptional careers are built in environments where employees are trusted, supported, and inspired to perform at their best. Successful graduates will enjoy numerous benefits throughout their professional journey. 1. Make an Impact from Day One Rather than spending months observing, graduates immediately contribute to meaningful projects that create value for clients, businesses, and communities. Participants gain practical experience while working alongside experienced professionals on real business challenges. 2. Build Future-Ready Skills The program equips graduates with practical and professional skills required to succeed in today s rapidly changing business environment. Participants strengthen competencies such as: Problem-solving Communication Leadership Collaboration Innovation Critical thinking Client engagement Professional consulting skills 3. Graduate Learning Academy One of the highlights of the program is Deloitte s Graduate Learning Academy. This structured learning experience includes: Interactive workshops Creative challenges Practical learning activities Professional coaching Skill development sessions Confidence-building exercises Networking opportunities The academy helps graduates establish strong foundations for long-term career success. 4. Dedicated Career Coaching Every graduate receives personalized career support through: A dedicated career coach Professional mentoring Peer support networks Continuous feedback Career guidance Personal development planning Graduates are never expected to navigate their professional journey alone. 5. Continuous Learning Opportunities Learning continues beyond onboarding. Participants have access to: E-learning platforms Professional development resources Ongoing mentoring Skills enhancement programs Career progression opportunities This commitment to continuous learning enables graduates to adapt and thrive throughout their careers. 6. Explore Diverse Career Paths Deloitte offers opportunities across numerous industries and business areas, allowing graduates to discover where their strengths and interests align. Participants can explore multiple professional pathways while building deep technical and industry expertise. Who Can Apply? The Graduate Program is intended for applicants who meet one of the following criteria: Students completing university studies between mid-2026 and the end of 2026. Graduates who completed their degree within the last two years. Individuals ready to begin a graduate career with Deloitte in 2027. Applicants from a wide variety of academic disciplines are encouraged to apply. What Makes Deloitte Different? Deloitte values diversity of thought, education, and experience. Rather than restricting career paths based on university degrees, Deloitte encourages graduates to pursue opportunities that match their interests, skills, and potential. This flexible approach allows graduates to develop careers that align with both their professional aspirations and evolving business needs. Skills Matcher Quiz Applicants who are unsure which Deloitte team best suits their interests can complete Deloitte s Skills Matcher Quiz. The quiz helps candidates identify career opportunities that align with their: Skills Interests Preferences Career aspirations This makes it easier to choose the most suitable graduate pathway before submitting an application. Application Deadline Applications open: 1 July 2026 Application deadline: 22 July 2026 Interested applicants should complete and submit their applications before the closing date. How to Apply Prospective applicants should: Explore Deloitte s available Graduate and Vacationer opportunities. Learn about the different business areas and roles. Complete the Skills Matcher Quiz if needed. Submit an online application before 22 July 2026 . Click here for more and to apply Starting your career with Deloitte offers far more than a graduate job—it provides an opportunity to learn, grow, build meaningful professional relationships, and contribute to impactful work while developing a successful long-term career. title: Indo-Pacific NGO Blended Finance Accelerator 2026: Receive Up to AUD 840,000 (Approximately USD 550,000) Plus Expert Acceleration Support to Scale Climate and Gender Equality Solutions Across the Indo-Pacific
summary: Organizations working to solve climate change and gender inequality often face one common challenge—accessing sufficient financing to scale their impact. While grants remain important, attracting private investment has become increasingly critical for achieving sustainable development goals at scale. To address this challenge, the Australian Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade (DFAT) , in partnership with Convergence Blended Finance , has launched the Indo-Pacific NGO Blended Finance Accelerator , a five-year initiative worth AUD 10 million . The programme is designed to help NGOs develop innovative blended finance solutions that unlock private investment for climate action and gender equality initiatives throughout the Indo-Pacific region. The Accelerator combines catalytic grant funding with technical acceleration support, enabling NGOs to transform innovative ideas into investment-ready financial vehicles capable of delivering measurable environmental and social impact. Application Deadline: 20 July 2026 What is the Indo-Pacific NGO Blended Finance Accelerator? The Indo-Pacific NGO Blended Finance Accelerator is a comprehensive programme that helps NGOs bridge the gap between development funding and private capital. Although blended finance has expanded significantly across the Indo-Pacific, there remains a shortage of investable projects capable of attracting commercial investors. NGOs possess deep local knowledge, technical expertise, and trusted community relationships, making them well positioned to design impactful financial solutions. However, they currently sponsor only a small percentage of blended finance transactions globally due to several institutional and financial barriers. The Accelerator seeks to change this by providing financial support, expert guidance, investor connections, and technical resources that enable NGOs to create scalable blended finance vehicles capable of attracting long-term investment. Total Programme Funding The programme is backed by a five-year AUD 10 million investment and provides both financial support and capacity-building services. Available catalytic grants include: Up to AUD 300,000 (approximately USD 200,000 ) for early-stage blended finance initiatives. Up to AUD 840,000 (approximately USD 550,000 ) for advanced-stage and scaling initiatives. All grants are disbursed in Australian Dollars (AUD). What is Blended Finance? Blended finance refers to the strategic use of public, philanthropic, or concessional funding to attract private investment into projects that generate both financial returns and positive development outcomes. Rather than relying solely on donor funding, blended finance combines different sources of capital to create sustainable financing mechanisms capable of delivering large-scale impact. Examples include: Investment funds Project finance vehicles Private companies Bonds Financing facilities Results-based financing mechanisms Other innovative financial instruments Programme Objectives The Accelerator aims to: Increase private investment for climate action. Strengthen financing for gender equality initiatives. Build a pipeline of investment-ready blended finance projects. Support NGOs in becoming investment managers and impact managers. Expand partnerships between NGOs and private investors. Deliver measurable environmental and social outcomes. Strengthen financial sustainability for development initiatives. Priority Impact Areas Applications should focus on one or both of the programme s major themes. Climate Action Projects may support: Climate change mitigation Climate adaptation Clean energy Sustainable cities Responsible consumption Life below water Life on land These areas align with Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs): SDG 7 – Affordable and Clean Energy SDG 11 – Sustainable Cities and Communities SDG 12 – Responsible Consumption and Production SDG 13 – Climate Action SDG 14 – Life Below Water SDG 15 – Life on Land Gender Equality Projects should demonstrate a strong commitment to gender equality through: Women s empowerment Financial inclusion Healthcare Education Gender-responsive investment models Gender integration throughout project design, implementation, and governance Applications should align with SDG 5 – Gender Equality . What Support Will Selected NGOs Receive? Successful applicants receive more than funding. The programme provides: 1. Catalytic Grant Funding Funding supports activities such as: Feasibility studies Legal structuring Financial modelling Vehicle design Investment pipeline development Fundraising Pilot implementation Governance development Impact measurement systems 2. Acceleration Support Participants also gain access to: Professional training Curated market intelligence Data and analytics Technical mentoring Peer-learning opportunities Investor introductions Ecosystem partnerships Ongoing feedback from blended finance experts Guidance on engaging commercial investors Types of Grants Available Stage 1: Scoping and Feasibility Maximum funding: Up to AUD 300,000 Suitable for NGOs that are exploring blended finance opportunities. Funding may support: Financial model development Vehicle design Legal structuring Market assessments Stakeholder consultations Investment pipeline development Governance framework design Initial impact assessment Applicants should already have: An initial theory of change Early scoping work Potential partners identified Stage 2: Proof of Concept and Pilot Maximum funding: Up to AUD 840,000 Funding supports: Investor outreach Legal refinement Governance improvements Impact framework development Pilot investments Investment pipeline expansion Applicants should demonstrate: Completed feasibility work Initial financial model Internal and external support Key implementation partners At least one soft or hard investor commitment Stage 3: Scale-Up and Expansion Maximum funding: Up to AUD 840,000 Funding may be used for: Vehicle expansion Operational scaling Additional fundraising Legal improvements Pipeline growth General Partner (GP) capital commitments Preparing vehicles for larger investment rounds Applicants should already have: An investment-ready or operational vehicle Anchor investors Committed implementation partners Defined NGO roles Eligible Financial Vehicles Projects may include: Investment funds Bonds Project finance structures Private companies Financing facilities Results-based financing mechanisms Other innovative blended finance instruments Countries Eligible for Project Implementation Projects must focus on one or more eligible Indo-Pacific countries, including: Bangladesh Bhutan Cambodia Indonesia Laos Mongolia Myanmar Nepal Pakistan Philippines Sri Lanka Timor-Leste Vietnam Eligible Pacific Island nations include: Cook Islands Federated States of Micronesia Fiji Kiribati Nauru Niue Papua New Guinea Republic of Palau Republic of the Marshall Islands Samoa Solomon Islands Tokelau Tonga Tuvalu Vanuatu Applicants may be based anywhere in the world, provided their proposed blended finance vehicle serves eligible Indo-Pacific countries. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must: Be an NGO applying independently or with consortium partners. Demonstrate a clear need for grant funding. Develop a blended finance solution capable of attracting private investment. Hold a significant implementation role. Obtain internal leadership approval. Target eligible Indo-Pacific countries. Focus on climate action, gender equality, or both. Present a credible pathway toward investment readiness and financial sustainability. Evaluation Criteria Applications will be assessed based on: Development Impact Projects should generate measurable environmental and social benefits, particularly for underserved populations including: Women Indigenous communities Persons with disabilities Communities in Least Developed Countries (LDCs) Frontier markets Scalability and Replicability Proposals should demonstrate potential to: Mobilize significant private investment Expand across multiple markets Serve as models for future blended finance initiatives Additionality Applicants should explain: Why blended finance is necessary How the project addresses financing gaps Why grant funding is essential Gender Integration Strong proposals integrate gender considerations throughout project design rather than treating gender as an additional feature. Investment Readiness Projects should demonstrate: Strong governance Financial sustainability Investor engagement Commercial viability Risk management Internal Organizational Commitment Applicants should show: Leadership support Dedicated implementation teams Alignment with organizational strategy Stakeholder Partnerships Preference is given to proposals with: Financial partners Implementation partners Regional stakeholder support Track Record Applicants should demonstrate: Relevant technical expertise Local implementation experience Strong partnerships Capacity to manage blended finance initiatives Why This Programme Matters Traditional grant funding alone cannot meet the growing financing needs for climate resilience and gender equality across the Indo-Pacific. By combining grants with technical expertise and private investment, the Indo-Pacific NGO Blended Finance Accelerator enables NGOs to create sustainable financing solutions that continue generating impact long after grant funding has ended. The programme also strengthens NGO capacity to engage commercial investors, manage innovative financial vehicles, and build scalable development solutions capable of transforming communities across the region. Key Dates Application Deadline: 20 July 2026 Interested NGOs are encouraged to prepare their concept notes well before the deadline to maximize their chances of success. Click here for more and to apply Click here for more global grant opportunities The post Indo-Pacific NGO Blended Finance Accelerator 2026: Receive Up to AUD 840,000 (Approximately USD 550,000) Plus Expert Acceleration Support to Scale Climate and Gender Equality Solutions Across the Indo-Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: GreenLight Fund Expression of Interest 2026: Receive Up to US$600,000 in Unrestricted Funding to Expand Your Nonprofit Across the United States
summary: Nonprofit organizations with proven, evidence-based programs often reach a stage where their impact has been demonstrated, but expanding into new communities requires significant financial investment, strategic planning, staffing support, and strong local partnerships. GreenLight Fund is addressing this challenge by inviting eligible U.S.-based nonprofit organizations to submit an Expression of Interest (EOI) for future funding and expansion opportunities. Through this initiative, selected organizations may receive up to US$600,000 in unrestricted funding over four years, together with comprehensive strategic support designed to help successful nonprofit models launch and thrive in new communities throughout the United States. Unlike many traditional grant opportunities that provide funding alone, GreenLight Fund combines long-term financial investment with practical implementation support through its nationally recognized Beyond the Check partnership model. Expression of Interest Deadline Expression of Interest Pilot Deadline: July 17, 2026 Organizations interested in joining GreenLight Fund s future partnership pipeline should submit their Expression of Interest before the deadline. It is important to note that submitting an Expression of Interest is not the same as submitting a full grant application. Rather, it allows GreenLight Fund to learn more about your organization and determine whether it aligns with current or future partnership opportunities. Organizations whose missions and programs align with GreenLight Fund s model may later be invited into additional review and selection stages. About GreenLight Fund GreenLight Fund is a national nonprofit organization that works alongside local communities across the United States to improve opportunities for inclusive prosperity. Rather than imposing predetermined solutions, GreenLight partners directly with communities to identify their most pressing unmet needs before selecting proven nonprofit organizations capable of addressing those challenges. The organization focuses on removing barriers that prevent individuals and families from achieving long-term economic mobility, particularly those rooted in racial and economic inequities. Today, GreenLight operates across multiple sites throughout the United States and continues expanding its national presence. Each year, every GreenLight site follows a structured community-driven process to identify one critical local challenge and invest in a nonprofit organization capable of delivering measurable and sustainable results. Funding Benefits Organizations selected into GreenLight Fund s portfolio receive far more than financial support. Successful nonprofits may receive: Up to US$600,000 in unrestricted funding over four years. Multi-year investment designed to support long-term growth. Strategic guidance throughout expansion into new communities. Access to GreenLight Fund s Beyond the Check support model. Coaching on organizational strategy. Leadership and staffing support. Assistance overcoming implementation challenges. Partnership development with local organizations. Connections to additional funding opportunities. Access to GreenLight s national network of community leaders. Support from experienced local advisory boards. Ongoing relationship-building with stakeholders. Assistance developing sustainable local operations. Long-term accountability and performance measurement. Because the funding is unrestricted, organizations have greater flexibility to invest resources where they are most needed to achieve successful expansion. What Makes GreenLight Fund Different? Many grant programs simply award funding and expect organizations to implement projects independently. GreenLight Fund takes a different approach. Through its Beyond the Check model, GreenLight works as a long-term strategic partner throughout the expansion journey. This includes: Strategic coaching Local relationship building Staffing guidance Partnership development Fundraising support Leadership mentoring Sustainability planning Performance measurement The objective is not only to help nonprofits launch in a new city but also to ensure they become sustainable, community-supported organizations capable of creating lasting impact. Priority Impact Areas Although each GreenLight community identifies its own local priorities annually, the organization commonly supports nonprofit solutions addressing: Housing stability Education Youth opportunity Workforce development Economic mobility Health and well-being Family support Community resilience Reducing racial disparities Reducing economic disparities Inclusive prosperity initiatives Organizations operating in one or more of these focus areas are encouraged to explore the opportunity. Who Should Apply? GreenLight Fund is seeking nonprofit organizations that are prepared to scale successful programs into additional communities. Strong candidates typically: Operate as U.S.-based nonprofit organizations. Have a proven, evidence-based model. Demonstrate measurable outcomes. Can clearly show the impact of their work. Are interested in expanding into new U.S. communities. Address challenges related to economic mobility and inclusive prosperity. Have leadership committed to long-term growth and partnership. Organizations still testing early-stage ideas or pilot programs may not yet be ready for this opportunity, as GreenLight is specifically looking for solutions with demonstrated success that can be replicated effectively in new locations. Understanding the GreenLight Method GreenLight Fund follows a structured five-step process to identify and scale nonprofit solutions. 1. Discover GreenLight works closely with local communities, advisory committees, residents, and stakeholders to identify unmet community needs using research, local data, and extensive listening sessions. 2. Scout Once priority challenges have been identified, GreenLight searches nationally for nonprofit organizations with proven models capable of addressing those needs. 3. Select Potential organizations undergo careful evaluation before one nonprofit is selected as the strongest local fit. 4. Invest Selected organizations receive unrestricted funding together with strategic support to establish and expand operations within the new community. 5. Measure GreenLight works with partner organizations to establish measurable goals and track both programmatic and financial performance throughout the partnership. This structured approach helps ensure that investments produce measurable outcomes and long-term community impact. The GreenLight Partnership Journey Organizations interested in partnering with GreenLight Fund can expect several stages throughout the selection process. Stage 1: Interest and Exploration Organizations submit an Expression of Interest indicating their desire to be considered for future partnership opportunities. Stage 2: Pipeline Qualification GreenLight reviews submitted information to determine whether the organization aligns with its partnership model. Qualified organizations may be invited to an introductory conversation. Stage 3: Diligence and Alignment Organizations may be asked to provide additional documentation and participate in deeper discussions regarding program effectiveness, expansion readiness, and organizational capacity. Stage 4: Community-Driven Selection Finalists may present their work before a Selection Advisory Committee and engage directly with local community stakeholders. Stage 5: Partnership and Investment Each year, GreenLight selects one organization per site to expand into a new community. Selected organizations enter a formal partnership agreement outlining funding, expectations, strategic support, and shared accountability. Why This Opportunity Matters Scaling nonprofit programs successfully requires much more than financial investment. Organizations often need: Strong local partnerships. Community trust. Leadership coaching. Sustainable staffing structures. Fundraising guidance. Strategic planning. Long-term implementation support. GreenLight Fund addresses all of these needs simultaneously through its integrated funding and partnership approach. For nonprofits with proven models ready to reach more communities, this opportunity offers one of the most comprehensive expansion support programs currently available in the United States. Important Notes Expression of Interest Deadline: July 17, 2026 Submitting an Expression of Interest does not constitute a formal application. GreenLight Fund staff will review submissions to determine organizational alignment. Organizations that demonstrate strong alignment may be contacted for additional conversations or future selection cycles. Selection is based on community priorities, organizational readiness, measurable impact, and alignment with GreenLight Fund s mission. How to Apply Eligible nonprofit organizations should complete the GreenLight Fund Expression of Interest Form before the July 17, 2026 deadline. Click here for more and to apply Click here for more global grant opportunities The post GreenLight Fund Expression of Interest 2026: Receive Up to US$600,000 in Unrestricted Funding to Expand Your Nonprofit Across the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Free SOUTHMOD Training Course 2026 on Tax and Benefit Microsimulation for Latin America | Hybrid Participation in Quito, Ecuador
summary: Professionals, researchers, policymakers, and academics interested in tax and social protection policy analysis are invited to register for the SOUTHMOD Training Course on Microsimulation for Tax and Benefit Analysis in Latin America , taking place 14–16 July 2026 in Quito, Ecuador . Organized by the United Nations University World Institute for Development Economics Research (UNU-WIDER) in collaboration with Facultad Latinoamericana de Ciencias Sociales (FLACSO Ecuador) , Universidad del Pacífico , the International Inequalities Institute at the London School of Economics (LSE) , and Pontificia Universidad Javeriana’s Observatorio Fiscal , this three-day hybrid training programme provides participants with practical skills in using tax-benefit microsimulation models to evaluate fiscal policies. The training is completely free of charge , open to all eligible participants, and will be delivered entirely in Spanish . Registration Status Registration is currently open. Training Dates 14 July 2026 – 16 July 2026 Venue Quito, Ecuador The course will be offered in a hybrid format , allowing participants to attend either in person or through virtual participation. About the SOUTHMOD Training Course The SOUTHMOD training course is designed to equip participants with practical knowledge and hands-on experience in using microsimulation models to analyze the impact of tax and social benefit policies across Latin America. Participants will learn how to evaluate how different fiscal policies affect: Household income Poverty levels Income inequality Government revenues Social protection outcomes Distribution of taxes and benefits The programme focuses on practical applications that support evidence-based policymaking and economic research. What is SOUTHMOD? SOUTHMOD is an internationally recognized research initiative that develops, updates, and maintains tax-benefit microsimulation models for low- and middle-income countries. These models combine: Household income data Household expenditure data National tax legislation Social benefit policies Using these datasets, researchers and policymakers can simulate proposed policy changes before implementation and estimate their likely economic and social impacts. This makes SOUTHMOD an essential tool for governments, researchers, and development organizations seeking to design more effective fiscal and social protection policies. Countries Covered by the Training This training focuses on four Latin American microsimulation models: ECUAMOD (Ecuador) COLMOD (Colombia) BOLMOD (Bolivia) PERUMOD (Peru) Participants will gain practical experience using these country-specific models to assess tax and benefit reforms. What Participants Will Learn Throughout the three-day programme, participants will receive practical instruction on how to: Use tax-benefit microsimulation software. Analyze household-level income and expenditure data. Evaluate fiscal policy reforms. Estimate poverty impacts. Measure inequality outcomes. Assess government revenue implications. Compare alternative tax and social benefit scenarios. Interpret simulation results for policymaking. The course emphasizes applied learning through real-world examples and hands-on exercises. Who Should Attend? The programme is intended for both experienced and first-time SOUTHMOD users. Ideal participants include: Government officials involved in fiscal policy. Public finance specialists. Social policy analysts. Academic researchers. University lecturers. Think tank professionals. Public institution analysts. Graduate students interested in tax and social policy. Anyone interested in evidence-based policymaking and fiscal analysis is encouraged to participate. Why Tax-Benefit Microsimulation Matters Tax-benefit microsimulation has become one of the most valuable tools for governments and researchers because it enables policymakers to test the likely consequences of policy reforms before implementation. These models help answer important policy questions, including: How will a proposed tax reform affect low-income households? Which social benefit programme is most effective at reducing poverty? What impact will policy changes have on government revenues? How will inequality change under different fiscal scenarios? Such evidence supports smarter, fairer, and more efficient public policy decisions. Global Reach of SOUTHMOD UNU-WIDER currently hosts fourteen tax-benefit microsimulation models covering countries across multiple regions. Africa Egypt Ethiopia Ghana Mozambique Rwanda Tanzania Uganda Zambia Latin America Bolivia Colombia Ecuador Peru Southeast Asia Viet Nam Other Zanzibar These models complement previously developed systems for South Africa and Namibia, making SOUTHMOD one of the world s leading tax-benefit microsimulation initiatives for developing economies. Organizing Institutions The training course is jointly organized by: United Nations University World Institute for Development Economics Research (UNU-WIDER) Facultad Latinoamericana de Ciencias Sociales (FLACSO Ecuador) Universidad del Pacífico International Inequalities Institute, London School of Economics (LSE) Pontificia Universidad Javeriana – Observatorio Fiscal This collaboration brings together leading experts in economics, public finance, social policy, and fiscal analysis. Benefits of Participating Participants will benefit from: Free professional training. Practical hands-on learning. Hybrid participation options. Exposure to internationally recognized policy tools. Improved analytical skills. Better understanding of tax-benefit modelling. Networking opportunities with researchers and policymakers. Knowledge applicable to fiscal policy design and evaluation. Enhanced research and policy analysis capabilities. Why You Should Register Whether you work in government, academia, public policy, or development research, this course offers an excellent opportunity to strengthen your expertise in tax-benefit microsimulation and evidence-based fiscal policymaking. By learning to use SOUTHMOD, participants will gain practical skills that can support better policy design, improve social protection systems, and contribute to reducing poverty and inequality across developing countries. Registration is free, and interested participants are encouraged to secure their place before the programme begins on 14 July 2026 . Apply Here For more information on this training opportunity visit here Discover more training opportunities on OFY here The post Free SOUTHMOD Training Course 2026 on Tax and Benefit Microsimulation for Latin America | Hybrid Participation in Quito, Ecuador appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: European Studies Review Job Fair 2026: Discover the Latest Internships, Traineeships, and Career Opportunities Across Europe
summary: Finding the right internship, traineeship, or entry-level job can be one of the most important steps toward building a successful international career. The European Studies Review (ESR) Job Fair brings together some of the latest opportunities offered by leading European institutions, international organizations, consulting firms, research institutes, and private-sector employers. Updated regularly and promoted every Wednesday on LinkedIn, the ESR Job Fair serves as a valuable resource for students, graduates, and young professionals seeking career opportunities in diplomacy, European Union affairs, economics, law, communications, finance, sustainability, research, and public policy. Whether you are looking to gain your first professional experience, develop specialized skills, or begin a long-term career within European institutions, this collection of opportunities provides an excellent starting point. Why Follow the European Studies Review Job Fair? The European Studies Review has become a trusted platform for promoting opportunities that help young professionals launch successful international careers. The Job Fair features vacancies from prestigious organizations across Europe, including European Union institutions, international organizations, consulting firms, financial institutions, research centers, and advocacy organizations. Applicants benefit from having multiple career opportunities available in one place, allowing them to explore positions that match their education, interests, and professional goals. Latest Opportunities Available 1. UNITAR – Multilateral Diplomacy Programme Traineeship Location: Geneva, Switzerland The United Nations Institute for Training and Research (UNITAR) is recruiting trainees for its Multilateral Diplomacy Programme. This opportunity provides participants with practical exposure to international diplomacy while supporting UNITAR s work in multilateral cooperation, global governance, and diplomatic capacity-building. Application Deadline: 8 July 2. European Central Bank – Financial Analysis Traineeship Location: Frankfurt am Main, Germany The European Central Bank (ECB) is offering a traineeship within the Directorate General Market Infrastructure and Payments. Successful candidates will gain valuable experience in financial analysis while working alongside experts responsible for Europe s financial infrastructure and payment systems. Application Deadline: 9 July 3. European Securities and Markets Authority (ESMA) – Multiple Traineeships Location: Paris, France ESMA is offering multiple traineeship positions for graduates interested in financial regulation, market supervision, and European financial governance. Participants will work alongside specialists responsible for maintaining the stability and integrity of European financial markets. Application Deadline: 15 July 4. European Economic and Social Committee – Traineeship Programme The European Economic and Social Committee offers traineeships that provide participants with practical experience in European policymaking while working within one of the European Union s advisory institutions. The programme allows trainees to understand how EU institutions operate and contribute to policy development. Application Deadline: 30 September 5. European Committee of the Regions – Traineeships The European Committee of the Regions offers traineeships for graduates interested in regional development, European governance, and local policymaking. Participants gain first-hand experience supporting one of the European Union s consultative bodies. Application Deadline: 30 September 6. British Chamber of Commerce (EU Belgium) – Committees Officer This position offers professionals an opportunity to support committee activities, stakeholder engagement, and organizational coordination within the British Chamber of Commerce. Application Deadline: 3 July 7. Avocats Sans Frontières – Research Assistant Intern Applicants interested in legal research, human rights, and international justice can apply for the Research Assistant Internship offered by Avocats Sans Frontières. The internship provides valuable practical experience supporting research activities within an international legal organization. Application Deadline: 12 July 8. Time Place Consulting – Advocacy Communication Intern Time Place Consulting is recruiting an Advocacy and Communication Intern. Successful applicants will gain hands-on experience supporting advocacy initiatives, strategic communication, stakeholder engagement, and policy communication. Application Deadline: 15 July 9. EIPA – EU Policy-Making Intern The European Institute of Public Administration (EIPA) offers an internship focused on European Union policy-making. Participants gain practical knowledge of EU governance, institutional processes, and policy implementation. Application Deadline: 15 July 10. EGMONT – Royal Institute for International Relations The Royal Institute for International Relations is offering internships within its Education and Training Programme. This opportunity is ideal for candidates interested in international affairs, diplomacy, security studies, and foreign policy. Application Deadline: 15 July 11. EUnited – Membership Communications Officer EUnited is recruiting a Membership and Communications Officer to support member engagement, communications strategies, and organizational activities. Application Deadline: 31 July 12. EY Belgium – Internship Payroll EY Belgium offers internship opportunities within its payroll team. Participants gain professional experience working within one of the world s leading professional services firms. Application Deadline: Rolling applications 13. Acumen Public Affairs – Trainee Consultant Opportunities Acumen Public Affairs is recruiting: Health Trainee Consultant Sustainability Trainee Consultant These opportunities allow graduates to gain consulting experience within health policy and sustainability sectors. Application Deadline: Rolling applications Benefits of Following the ESR Job Fair Applicants can benefit from: Access to multiple internship, traineeship, and job opportunities in one place. Opportunities with prestigious European institutions and international organizations. Career development in diplomacy, finance, communications, public policy, law, economics, sustainability, and international relations. Practical professional experience. Exposure to European policymaking and governance. Networking opportunities with leading professionals. A convenient weekly source of newly published career opportunities. Why You Should Apply Many of these organizations are internationally recognized and provide excellent environments for learning, professional development, and long-term career growth. Whether your ambition is to work within European Union institutions, international organizations, financial institutions, research institutes, or consulting firms, these opportunities can serve as important stepping stones toward achieving your career goals. Application Deadlines The opportunities featured in this Job Fair have different application deadlines: British Chamber of Commerce – 3 July UNITAR – 8 July European Central Bank – 9 July Avocats Sans Frontières – 12 July ESMA – 15 July Time Place Consulting – 15 July EIPA – 15 July EGMONT – 15 July EUnited – 31 July European Economic and Social Committee – 30 September European Committee of the Regions – 30 September EY Belgium – Rolling Deadline Acumen Public Affairs – Rolling Deadline Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their applications well before the stated deadlines. How to Stay Updated European Studies Review publishes new career opportunities every Wednesday through its Job Fair. Follow the platform to stay informed about internships, traineeships, graduate programmes, fellowships, and job openings across Europe. title: Bank of America Campus Experience 2026: Launch Your Career with World-Class Internships, Training, Mentorship, and Global Networking Opportunities
summary: Starting your professional career with one of the world s leading financial institutions can significantly shape your future. The Bank of America Campus Experience offers students and early-career professionals an outstanding opportunity to gain real-world experience, receive mentorship from industry experts, develop leadership skills, and build a strong professional network within one of the most respected global banking institutions. Whether you are looking for an internship or your first full-time position after graduation, Bank of America provides a structured career development experience designed to help young professionals transition successfully from academic life into the corporate world. If you re ambitious, driven, eager to learn, and ready to challenge yourself, the Bank of America Campus Experience could be the perfect opportunity to begin your career. Why Choose the Bank of America Campus Experience? Beginning a career is about much more than simply finding a job. It is about joining an organization that invests in your long-term growth and provides you with the tools necessary to succeed. At Bank of America, students are welcomed into a collaborative environment where learning, innovation, and professional development are prioritized from day one. The Campus Experience is carefully designed to provide participants with meaningful work assignments while supporting their personal and professional development throughout the program. What Makes the Campus Experience Unique? Participants enjoy access to a wide range of career development resources, including: 1. Supportive Mentorship Participants receive guidance from experienced professionals working in their chosen field of interest. Mentors help students: Understand the banking industry Develop professional skills Navigate workplace challenges Build confidence Explore future career opportunities Having access to experienced professionals allows participants to accelerate their learning and gain valuable career advice. 2. Industry-Leading Training Bank of America complements classroom education with practical learning experiences. Participants receive training that helps them: Strengthen technical knowledge Improve business communication Develop analytical thinking Build leadership capabilities Learn industry best practices Prepare for long-term career success The goal is to help students develop skills that extend far beyond what they learned in school. 3. Strong Culture and Community The Campus Experience encourages collaboration and relationship-building among interns and early-career professionals. Participants become part of a cohort of fellow students where they can: Learn together Collaborate on projects Share experiences Build lasting friendships Support one another throughout the program This collaborative environment helps create an engaging and enjoyable workplace experience. 4. Extensive Networking Opportunities Networking is an essential part of career growth. Bank of America gives participants opportunities to connect with professionals from across the organization. Networking opportunities include: Business leaders Experienced professionals Fellow interns Graduate hires Cross-functional teams Company-wide events These connections can become valuable professional relationships throughout your career. 5. Award-Winning Onboarding Experience New participants receive a welcoming orientation designed to make the transition into the workplace as smooth as possible. The onboarding process introduces participants to: Company values Workplace expectations Available resources Career development opportunities Professional support systems This helps new hires feel confident and prepared from the beginning. 6. Inclusive Workplace Environment Bank of America promotes an inclusive culture where every employee is encouraged to bring their authentic self to work. The organization values diversity and seeks to create an environment where everyone feels respected, supported, and able to contribute meaningfully. 7. Exclusive Events for Early Career Professionals Students participating in the Campus Experience can enjoy events specifically organized for interns and entry-level employees. These activities provide opportunities for learning, networking, collaboration, and engagement beyond everyday work responsibilities. 8. Additional Employee Perks Participants may also enjoy various unique experiences throughout the program, including: Social gatherings Team-building activities Fun giveaways Community events Special celebrations These activities contribute to a positive workplace culture while helping participants build relationships across the organization. Who Should Apply? The Campus Experience is designed for students and recent graduates who are eager to launch successful careers within the financial services industry. Ideal candidates are individuals who are: Motivated Hardworking Curious Ambitious Ready to learn Interested in professional growth Comfortable working in collaborative environments Career Opportunities Available Bank of America offers both: Internship opportunities Entry-level positions Available roles vary by country, business area, and recruitment cycle. Applicants are encouraged to explore opportunities available within their region. Why Bank of America? Bank of America is recognized globally as one of the world s leading financial institutions. Joining the organization provides access to: Global career opportunities Professional mentorship Continuous learning Leadership development Diverse teams International business exposure Long-term career advancement The organization is committed to helping young professionals build rewarding careers through structured development programs and meaningful work experiences. Equal Employment Opportunity Bank of America is committed to equal employment opportunity. Employment decisions are made without discrimination based on factors including: Race Religion Color Sex Sexual orientation Gender identity Gender expression Age National origin Citizenship Veteran status Disability Other legally protected characteristics The company actively promotes diversity, inclusion, and equal access to employment opportunities. Flexible Workplace Expectations Depending on the role, employees may be expected to work from the office while benefiting from role-specific flexibility. Specific attendance expectations are communicated during the hiring process and may vary by position. Reasonable accommodations are available for eligible employees based on disability or sincerely held religious beliefs. How to Apply Interested applicants should: Visit the official Bank of America Careers website. Explore available internship and student opportunities. Review the eligibility requirements for their region. Select a suitable program or position. Submit an online application through the Bank of America Careers portal. Applicants who applied previously may also be eligible to apply again depending on the specific recruitment cycle and program requirements. Application Deadline No application deadline has been specified. Since recruitment timelines vary by program and location, interested applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible when suitable positions become available. Official Website Learn more and apply through the official Bank of America Careers portal: Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Internship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post Bank of America Campus Experience 2026: Launch Your Career with World-Class Internships, Training, Mentorship, and Global Networking Opportunities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026–2027: Fully Funded Three-Year UK Research Fellowship for Early-Career Humanities and Social Sciences Researchers
summary: The British Academy has officially opened applications for its prestigious Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026 —one of the United Kingdom s leading funding opportunities for outstanding early-career researchers in the humanities and social sciences. Designed to help postdoctoral scholars establish independent research careers, the fellowship provides three years of funding to enable recipients to complete a significant piece of publishable research while strengthening their academic profile within a UK university or recognised research institution. Funded by the UK Department for Science, Innovation Technology (DSIT) , the fellowship supports researchers in developing their research and teaching experience, expanding their professional networks, and improving their prospects of securing permanent academic positions. If you are an early-career researcher looking to advance your academic career through high-quality independent research, the British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026 could be the ideal opportunity. About the British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowships The British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowships are awarded annually to an outstanding cohort of early-career researchers working in the humanities and social sciences. The scheme is specifically designed to: Support outstanding postdoctoral researchers as they establish independent academic careers. Enable fellows to complete a substantial piece of publishable research. Enhance research and teaching experience within a university environment. Strengthen fellows academic profiles and career prospects. Integrate fellows into communities of established scholars in their disciplines. The programme provides recipients with the time, resources, mentorship, and institutional support needed to become future academic leaders. Unlike short-term research grants, this fellowship offers sustained support over three years, allowing researchers to focus on producing high-quality research outputs while gaining valuable academic experience. Purpose of the Fellowship The British Academy created this fellowship to bridge the gap between completing a doctorate and securing a permanent academic position. The programme helps early-career researchers by: Developing independent research careers. Supporting publication of significant academic work. Encouraging excellence in humanities and social sciences research. Building teaching and research experience. Expanding academic networks. Improving long-term employability within higher education. The fellowship recognizes that the years immediately following a doctorate are often critical for developing an academic career. Fellowship Duration Successful applicants will receive funding for: Three years The fellowship is: Non-renewable Full-time Hosted at an eligible UK institution The earliest fellowship start date is: 1 September 2027 Funding Information The British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowship operates under the Full Economic Costing (FEC) model. Under this funding arrangement: The British Academy funds 80% of eligible Full Economic Costs. Funding includes salary costs. Funding also covers directly allocated and indirect research costs under the FEC framework. The fellowship is funded by the: UK Department for Science, Innovation Technology (DSIT). What Fellows Will Do Throughout the three-year fellowship, award holders will: Conduct an independent research project. Produce significant publishable research. Build an academic publication record. Gain valuable teaching experience. Participate in the scholarly community of their host institution. Collaborate with established academics. Strengthen professional research networks. Develop skills needed for long-term academic careers. The primary emphasis of the fellowship is on completing research that contributes meaningfully to the humanities or social sciences. Research Areas Supported The fellowship supports research within: Humanities Examples include: History Philosophy Literature Languages Archaeology Classics Religious Studies Cultural Studies Linguistics Social Sciences Examples include: Sociology Political Science Economics Human Geography Anthropology Education Psychology (where eligible) International Relations Public Policy Applicants should ensure that their proposed research falls within the British Academy s funding remit. Benefits of the British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowships Successful fellows receive numerous professional and academic benefits. These include: Financial Support Funding under the Full Economic Costing model. 80% funding of eligible salary costs. Support for eligible research-related costs. Research Development Fellows gain opportunities to: Conduct independent research. Produce high-impact publications. Complete major research projects. Develop long-term research agendas. Academic Experience Recipients benefit from: Teaching opportunities. Research collaboration. Integration into academic departments. Access to university research resources. Career Advancement The fellowship significantly strengthens applicants prospects for: Permanent academic appointments. Future research grants. International collaborations. Leadership roles in academia. Professional Networking Participants become part of: Academic research communities. Established scholarly networks. Collaborative research environments. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must carefully review all eligibility requirements before applying. To qualify, applicants must satisfy the following conditions. 1. Research Discipline Applicants must be researchers in: Humanities Social Sciences 2. Host Institution Applicants must be based at an eligible: UK university UK recognised research organisation for the full duration of the fellowship. 3. Nationality Requirements Applicants are eligible if they are: British citizens Nationals of any European Economic Area (EEA) country, regardless of where they obtained their doctorate OR Applicants of any nationality are eligible if they: Hold a doctorate awarded by a UK university. Applicants who do not fall into either category may still be eligible if they can demonstrate: A strong prior association with the UK academic community. 4. Career Stage Applicants must be early-career researchers. Specifically, applicants are expected to have completed their: Viva Voce examination between 1 April 2024 and 1 April 2027. Applicants must also already hold postdoctoral status when the Research Awards Committee meets. 5. Permanent Academic Posts Applicants are not eligible if they have ever held: A permanent academic appointment At any higher education institution Either in the UK or overseas This restriction also applies if the appointment occurred before the doctorate was awarded. Certain exemptions may apply and require prior approval from the British Academy before submitting an application. Please check last round s scheme guidance notes further information regarding exemption from this criterion. Host Institution Requirements The fellowship must be held at an eligible institution such as: UK universities UK recognised research organisations The host institution will provide the academic environment in which fellows complete their research. Referee Requirements Applicants must nominate one referee . The referee will complete a questionnaire through the Flexi-Grant® system. Normally, the referee should be: The external doctoral examiner If unavailable: Another suitably independent external academic. The referee cannot be: Proposed mentor Doctoral supervisor Internal examiner Staff member from the proposed host institution Application Process Applications are submitted online using the British Academy s: Grant Management System (GMS) – Flexi-Grant® Applicants are encouraged to prepare carefully before submitting. Application Timeline Applicants should note the following important dates. Activity Date Applications Open 1 July 2026 Application Deadline 30 September 2026 (17:00 BST) Earliest Fellowship Start 1 September 2027 Fellowship Duration Three Years Late submissions will not be accepted. How to Apply Applicants should: Review the official Scheme Guidance. Read the Application Guidance Notes carefully. Confirm eligibility. Secure an eligible UK host institution. Identify an eligible referee. Prepare the research proposal. Submit the application through Flexi-Grant® before the deadline. Ensure institutional approval is completed before the submission deadline. Scheme Guidance Before applying, applicants are strongly encouraged to review: Scheme Guidance Notes Application Guidance Notes Eligibility requirements Funding rules Assessment criteria Carefully reviewing these documents can significantly improve the quality of an application. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) The British Academy provides a Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) section to help applicants understand the fellowship and application process. Common topics covered include: Eligibility requirements Career stage requirements Host institution eligibility Referee requirements Funding arrangements Application process Research proposal guidance Fellowship conditions Applicants are encouraged to review the FAQs before contacting the British Academy. Applicant Support Applicants who require assistance can access several support resources. These include: Scheme Guidance Notes Application Guidance Notes Frequently Asked Questions Knowbot AI chatbot British Academy Research Funding Team Questions can also be directed via email to: posts@thebritishacademy.ac.uk Why Apply for the British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowship? Securing a permanent academic position has become increasingly competitive. The British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowship provides a unique opportunity to strengthen an academic career by offering dedicated time, funding, and institutional support to conduct independent research. Beyond financial support, fellows gain valuable experience in research, teaching, collaboration, and scholarly engagement—helping them build competitive academic portfolios and establish themselves within their disciplines. For researchers seeking to publish influential work, develop new collaborations, and secure future academic opportunities, this fellowship represents one of the UK s most prestigious early-career funding schemes. Visit HERE to learn more about the British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowships. Conclusion The British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026 provide exceptional early-career researchers in the humanities and social sciences with the opportunity to establish themselves as independent scholars through a fully supported three-year research programme. Hosted at eligible UK universities and research organisations, the fellowship enables recipients to complete significant publishable research, gain valuable teaching and research experience, and integrate into leading academic communities. With applications opening on 1 July 2026 and closing on 30 September 2026 at 17:00 BST , eligible researchers should begin preparing their proposals, confirming host institution arrangements, securing an appropriate referee, and reviewing the official guidance documents well before the deadline. For ambitious postdoctoral researchers aiming to strengthen their academic careers and contribute impactful research to the humanities and social sciences, this fellowship offers an outstanding pathway toward long-term academic success. Discover more global Fellowship and Doctoral opportunities on OFY. The post British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026–2027: Fully Funded Three-Year UK Research Fellowship for Early-Career Humanities and Social Sciences Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Free UN Online Course on Gender and Environment with Digital Certificate – Open for Global Learners
summary: The UN CC:Learn Open Online Course on Gender and Environment is currently accepting enrollments from learners around the world. This comprehensive self-paced course provides participants with an in-depth understanding of the relationship between gender equality and environmental sustainability , while equipping them with practical tools to integrate gender-responsive approaches into environmental policies, projects, and development initiatives. Developed through a collaboration between leading international organizations, including the Global Environment Facility (GEF) , the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) , and UNITAR s UN CC:Learn , the course serves as an important learning resource for individuals seeking to strengthen their knowledge of climate action, biodiversity conservation, sustainable development, and environmental governance. Unlike traditional classroom programs, this online course allows learners to study at their own pace from anywhere in the world. Participants can access learning materials whenever convenient, making it suitable for both students and working professionals. Why This Course Matters Environmental challenges such as climate change, biodiversity loss, land degradation, pollution, and waste management do not affect everyone equally. Women, men, girls, and boys often experience environmental issues differently because of varying social, economic, and cultural roles. Understanding these differences is becoming increasingly important for governments, NGOs, development agencies, researchers, and businesses seeking to design inclusive environmental policies and sustainable development strategies. This course helps participants understand: Why gender equality contributes to better environmental outcomes. How environmental degradation affects different populations differently. The importance of women s leadership and empowerment in climate action. How gender-responsive policies improve conservation and sustainable development. International frameworks that support gender equality in environmental governance. What You Will Learn The course provides practical and theoretical knowledge covering several major environmental themes. Participants will learn: The relationship between gender equality and environmental sustainability. Global environmental agreements that include gender considerations. How gender-responsive policies improve environmental outcomes. Practical approaches to integrating gender into environmental projects. Ways to promote women s empowerment within biodiversity conservation. Gender considerations in climate change adaptation and mitigation. Gender issues relating to international waters. Sustainable land degradation management. Chemicals and waste management from a gender perspective. Course Structure The learning programme consists of six comprehensive modules , each designed to build practical knowledge step by step. The modules include: Introduction Climate Change International Waters Biodiversity Land Degradation Chemicals and Waste Each module takes approximately one hour to complete, resulting in a total learning time of around six hours . Since the course is self-paced, learners can complete it according to their own schedules without fixed deadlines. Who Should Enroll? The course has been designed for a wide variety of learners, including: Students studying environmental sciences or sustainable development. Climate change practitioners. Biodiversity specialists. Government officials working on environmental policies. NGO staff involved in environmental conservation. Development practitioners. Researchers. Community leaders. Sustainability consultants. Anyone interested in learning how gender equality contributes to environmental sustainability. Certification Participants who successfully complete all quizzes with a minimum score of 70% qualify for a UN Digital Certificate of Completion . Important certification information: A minimum score of 70% is required on every quiz. Participants have three attempts for each quiz. The course itself is free. The official digital certificate becomes available upon payment of a USD 20 certification fee after successfully passing all quizzes. Languages Available The course is available in multiple languages, making it accessible to learners worldwide: English Spanish French International Partners The course has been developed through collaboration among several globally recognized organizations, including: Global Environment Facility (GEF) United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) UN CC:Learn International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN) UN Women United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP) Convention on Biological Diversity United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC) United Nations Convention to Combat Desertification (UNCCD) Basel, Rotterdam and Stockholm Conventions, among others. Benefits of Taking the Course Participants will benefit from: Free access to high-quality UN learning materials. Flexible self-paced online learning. Practical knowledge applicable in environmental and development work. Improved understanding of global gender and environmental frameworks. Enhanced professional skills in sustainability and climate action. Opportunity to earn a recognized UN Digital Certificate. Better preparation for careers in environmental policy, NGOs, international organizations, research, and sustainable development. Application Deadline There is currently no application deadline. Enrollment remains open, and interested learners may register and begin the course at any time. How to Apply Interested applicants can register online through the official UN CC:Learn course page : Discover more global short courses opportunities on OFY here The post Free UN Online Course on Gender and Environment with Digital Certificate – Open for Global Learners appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: German Bundestag International Parliamentary Scholarship (IPS) 2026 for U.S. Citizens: Fully Funded Five-Month Fellowship in Berlin with Monthly Stipend, Free Accommodation and Travel Support
summary: Young leaders passionate about democracy, governance, public policy, and international affairs now have an outstanding opportunity to gain first-hand experience inside one of Europe s most influential democratic institutions. Applications are officially open for the International Parliamentary Scholarship (IPS) Programme 2026 , an initiative of the German Bundestag that enables outstanding young graduates to spend five months in Berlin while learning about Germany s parliamentary system and participating directly in legislative work. The International Parliamentary Scholarship (IPS) is one of Germany s flagship international exchange programmes designed to strengthen democratic values, encourage civic engagement, and foster long-term cooperation between Germany and partner countries. Established in 1986 , the programme has provided thousands of young professionals from approximately 50 countries with practical parliamentary experience while building an international network of future leaders committed to democracy and public service. For applicants in the United States, the programme is coordinated through the German Missions and offers selected participants the unique opportunity to work alongside a Member of the German Bundestag while also taking part in an extensive academic and professional development programme. About the International Parliamentary Scholarship (IPS) The International Parliamentary Scholarship is organized by the German Bundestag in cooperation with three of Germany s leading universities: Freie Universität Berlin Humboldt-Universität zu Berlin Technische Universität Berlin Together, these institutions deliver a comprehensive programme that combines academic learning, professional training, seminars, networking opportunities, and practical parliamentary experience. The fellowship is specifically designed for talented young graduates who have demonstrated a commitment to democratic principles and civic participation in their home countries. Through the programme, participants gain valuable insights into parliamentary decision-making, political institutions, governance structures, and international cooperation. What Makes the IPS Programme Unique? Unlike traditional academic scholarships, the IPS Programme offers participants the opportunity to experience politics from inside the German Parliament. Participants spend five months in Berlin , where they become immersed in Germany s democratic institutions and political culture. One of the highlights of the programme is a three-month internship placement with a Member of the German Bundestag , allowing fellows to observe parliamentary work closely while contributing to daily legislative activities. This practical experience is complemented by university courses, professional seminars, workshops, and political training sessions that deepen participants understanding of democratic governance. The programme aims not only to educate participants but also to encourage international dialogue and strengthen democratic cooperation between Germany and participating countries. Fellowship Benefits Selected fellows receive a comprehensive financial package that allows them to fully concentrate on learning and professional development throughout the programme. Benefits include: Monthly scholarship of 700 Euros Free accommodation throughout the fellowship Travel expenses Health insurance coverage Practical experience working in the German Parliament Participation in seminars and professional training sessions University courses delivered by leading Berlin universities Networking opportunities with political leaders, scholars, and fellow participants from around the world Exposure to Germany s parliamentary decision-making processes International leadership and intercultural experience Programme Duration The International Parliamentary Scholarship runs annually from: March 1 to July 31 During these five months, participants complete academic, professional, and practical parliamentary activities designed to prepare them for future leadership roles in government, public administration, international organizations, academia, civil society, and policy development. Eligibility Requirements Applicants from the United States must meet all of the following requirements: Hold U.S. citizenship Possess a completed college degree Demonstrate very good German language proficiency (minimum B2 level) Have a good understanding of German politics, society, and history Be under the age of 30 at the beginning of the fellowship Demonstrate a genuine interest in democratic governance and parliamentary systems Show active engagement in promoting democratic values and civil society The programme is particularly suited to graduates interested in careers in diplomacy, public administration, political science, international relations, law, public policy, journalism, governance, and civic leadership. Selection Process Applications undergo a competitive pre-selection process. Shortlisted candidates are invited to participate in interviews conducted within the United States. Final scholarship recipients are selected by an independent panel appointed by the German Bundestag. The rigorous selection process ensures that successful applicants possess strong academic credentials, leadership potential, commitment to democratic values, and the ability to serve as ambassadors for international cooperation. Why You Should Apply Participating in the International Parliamentary Scholarship offers far more than international study. It provides an opportunity to: Gain real-world parliamentary experience Build international leadership skills Develop professional networks across Germany and beyond Learn directly from legislators and political experts Enhance intercultural communication skills Strengthen your understanding of democratic institutions Improve your career prospects in government, international organizations, NGOs, academia, and public policy The IPS Programme has become a globally respected fellowship because it combines academic excellence with practical political experience, creating future leaders who contribute to democratic development in their own countries. Application Deadline The deadline to submit applications for the International Parliamentary Scholarship (IPS) Programme 2026 is: 31 July 2025 Interested applicants should prepare their documents well before the deadline to ensure they have sufficient time to complete the application process. How to Apply Applicants should review the official programme information and submit their applications through the German Bundestag s official IPS application portal . Click here for Official Programme Information: Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here The post German Bundestag International Parliamentary Scholarship (IPS) 2026 for U.S. Citizens: Fully Funded Five-Month Fellowship in Berlin with Monthly Stipend, Free Accommodation and Travel Support appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNESCO Project Associate – Social and Human Sciences Job in India 2026: Apply for a 12-Month Position in New Delhi Before 26 July
summary: The United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO) has announced an exciting career opportunity for professionals interested in advancing social development, gender equality, and human rights through international cooperation. Applications are now open for the Project Associate – Social and Human Sciences position based in New Delhi, India , offering successful candidates the opportunity to contribute to high-impact projects that empower communities and promote inclusive development. This position is hosted by UNESCO s Social and Human Sciences (SHS) Sector , one of the organization s key programme areas dedicated to promoting social inclusion, ethics, gender equality, youth empowerment, human rights, and peaceful societies. The successful candidate will support the implementation of the UNESCO–NIKE Kickstart Her Future – India Project , alongside other extra-budgetary initiatives within the SHS Sector. The appointment is offered under a Service Contract (SC6) for an initial duration of 12 months , with the duty station located in New Delhi, India . The vacancy is open to external candidates , providing professionals from relevant backgrounds with an opportunity to join one of the world s leading United Nations agencies. About UNESCO UNESCO is a specialized agency of the United Nations that works to build peace through international cooperation in education, science, culture, communication, and the social and human sciences. Through its programmes, UNESCO supports sustainable development, promotes human rights, strengthens education systems, preserves cultural heritage, and advances scientific collaboration across the globe. About the Project The selected Project Associate will play an important role in supporting the implementation of the UNESCO–NIKE Kickstart Her Future – India initiative. The project focuses on empowering women and girls by strengthening opportunities for participation, leadership, and social inclusion through collaborative programmes and partnerships. Working under the supervision of the Programme Specialist and Chief of the Social and Human Sciences Sector, the successful candidate will provide programme, administrative, financial, procurement, and coordination support to ensure successful project delivery. Key Responsibilities Successful applicants will contribute to several important functions, including: Supporting programme planning and implementation. Coordinating project activities and monitoring progress. Assisting with financial administration and budget tracking. Supporting procurement processes and contract administration. Organizing workshops, stakeholder consultations, meetings, and project events. Preparing reports, correspondence, presentations, and project documentation. Maintaining project records and ensuring timely reporting. Liaising with government institutions, implementing partners, NGOs, consultants, and other stakeholders. Supporting communication among project partners. Performing other administrative and coordination duties assigned by supervisors. Why You Should Apply Working with UNESCO provides professionals with an opportunity to contribute directly to international development while building valuable experience within the United Nations system. Some of the advantages of this position include: Opportunity to work with a globally respected UN agency. Professional experience in international development programmes. Exposure to large-scale gender equality and social inclusion initiatives. Collaboration with government institutions, civil society organizations, and international partners. Development of project management, financial management, procurement, and stakeholder engagement skills. Opportunity to contribute to projects supporting women and youth empowerment. International networking opportunities with development professionals. Required Qualifications Applicants should possess: A university degree in a relevant field such as Social Sciences, Development Studies, Gender Studies, Public Administration, Management, Business Administration, International Relations, Finance, Commerce, or related disciplines. At least three years of relevant professional experience in programme management, project administration, finance, procurement, coordination, or related work. Excellent English communication skills. Strong organizational and analytical abilities. Good knowledge of Microsoft Office applications. Ability to work effectively with diverse stakeholders and multicultural teams. Core Values UNESCO seeks candidates who demonstrate its core organizational values: Commitment to the Organization Integrity Respect for Diversity Professionalism Contract Details The successful candidate will receive: Position: Project Associate – Social and Human Sciences Contract Type: Service Contract (SC6) Duration: 12 months Duty Station: New Delhi, India Work Arrangement: On-site Hiring Unit: Social and Human Sciences Sector (SHS) Application Deadline The deadline for submitting applications is: 26 July 2026 (Midnight UTC−5). How to Apply Interested candidates should submit their applications through the official UNESCO Careers portal before the application deadline. Applicants are encouraged to carefully review the job description, eligibility requirements, and required application documents before submitting their applications on official website .
